meta.subject: Social Studies
meta.topicTitle: Location of Africa on the Map of the World

rewritePrinciple: Rewrite Topic 1 from evidence notes. Remove or quarantine unsupported filler. Keep learner modules full, direct and traceable.

officialSubtopicBlueprint.officialSubtopic: Location and position of Africa using major lines of latitudes and longitudes

recommendedModules.title: Africa on the World Map
requiredTeachingPoints: Africa is a continent on the world map.
P7 learners study Africa in relation to the whole world.
Africa is located using compass directions, latitude and longitude.

recommendedModules.title: Latitude, Longitude and Position
requiredTeachingPoints: Meaning and use of latitude.
Meaning and use of longitude.
Meaning of position in map work.
Difference between latitude and longitude.
recommendedModules.extraSourceReason: Official pages require the words and teacher guidance, but do not give full definitions. Use an approved geography/map source or teacher guide for final definitions.

recommendedModules.title: Equator, Tropics and Prime Meridian
requiredTeachingPoints: Identify the Equator on a world map.
Identify the Prime Meridian on a world map.
Identify the Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn as major latitude lines crossing Africa.
Show lines of latitude and longitude on a map of Africa.

recommendedModules.title: Locating Africa Using Compass Directions and Lines
requiredTeachingPoints: Use compass directions to state where oceans, seas and continents are from Africa.
Use latitude and longitude to locate Africa and countries on maps.
Answer map-location questions in complete statements.

recommendedModules.title: Map Drawing Activity: Africa, Regions and Lines
requiredTeachingPoints: Draw a simple map of Africa.
Show regions on the map.
Show lines of latitude and longitude.

recommendedModules.title: Continents of the World
requiredTeachingPoints: Name the seven continents.
Recognise Africa as one of the continents.
Read and spell continent names correctly.

recommendedModules.title: Continents in Order of Size
requiredTeachingPoints: Order: Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica/Antarctic, Europe, Australia.
Africa is the second largest continent.

recommendedModules.title: Oceans and Seas Around Africa
requiredTeachingPoints: Name the oceans in the syllabus list.
Name the Mediterranean Sea and Red Sea.
Identify water bodies that surround Africa.

recommendedModules.title: Compass Directions from Africa
requiredTeachingPoints: State directions of oceans from Africa.
State directions of seas from Africa.
State directions of other continents from Africa.
recommendedModules.extraSourceReason: Directions need a verified map/globe source before exact direction statements are final.

recommendedModules.title: Regions of Africa
requiredTeachingPoints: West Africa, East Africa, Northern Africa, Central Africa, Southern Africa and Horn of Africa.
Identify the regions that make up Africa.

recommendedModules.title: Locating African Regions on a Map
requiredTeachingPoints: Show regions on a map of Africa.
Use region names in written answers.
recommendedModules.extraSourceReason: Exact regional boundaries/countries need a verified map or approved learner source.

officialSubtopicBlueprint.officialSubtopic: Countries in each region and their capital cities

recommendedModules.title: Countries and Capital Cities by Region
requiredTeachingPoints: List countries in each African region.
List capital cities of respective countries.
Read, spell and write region/country/capital names.
recommendedModules.extraSourceReason: Official pages require countries and capitals but do not provide a complete table. A verified atlas/approved book/source is required.

recommendedModules.title: Islands Which Are Part of Africa
requiredTeachingPoints: Name islands which are part of Africa.
Locate islands in relation to Africa where possible.
recommendedModules.extraSourceReason: Official pages require islands but do not list island names.

currentAppModulesToReviewOrRemove.reason: Longitude time calculation is not supported by current Topic 1 evidence notes. Needs approved source or removal/quarantine as extension.

Uses/importances/advantages/disadvantages of continents are not required by the extracted NCDC Topic 1 content. Replace with source-backed continent identification/size practice unless another source justifies it.
The official Topic 1 requirement is naming surrounding water bodies and directions. Uses/importance may be useful but need source justification and should not crowd out required map skills.
Marine gateways/channels are not in the extracted Topic 1 content. Needs source justification or removal.
Not in extracted Topic 1 content. Needs source justification or removal.
Regions are required for identification/map work; uses/importance framing is unsupported by current evidence.
Capital cities are required for naming/listing; uses/importances need source support and may be unnecessary filler.
May be useful but not explicitly required in extracted Topic 1 evidence. Needs source support or later extension status.
